text:
Good evening. You made it to the end of another day.

This is your space — no performance, no judgment. Just a quiet conversation with yourself.

Let's see how today really went.
If today were a weather report, what would it be?
options: Sunny — things flowed
Cloudy — low energy, unclear
Stormy — rough and draining
Mixed — patches of both
answer=Sunny — things flowed|Mixed — patches of both:A1_Q_AGENCY_HIGH
answer=Cloudy — low energy, unclear|Stormy — rough and draining:A1_Q_AGENCY_LOW

text: You described today as "{A1_OPEN.answer}". When something went well today, what was the main reason?
options: I had prepared for it
I adapted when it shifted
The timing was just right
Someone else made it happen

text: You described today as "{A1_OPEN.answer}". When things got difficult, what did you find yourself doing?
options: Looked for what I could still control
Waited for things to change on their own
Pushed through but felt alone in it
Got frustrated at circumstances or people
answer=I had prepared for it|I adapted when it shifted:A1_Q_CHOICE_INT
answer=The timing was just right|Someone else made it happen:A1_Q_CHOICE_EXT
answer=Looked for what I could still control|Pushed through but felt alone in it:A1_Q_CHOICE_INT
answer=Waited for things to change on their own|Got frustrated at circumstances or people:A1_Q_CHOICE_EXT

text: Think of one moment today where things didn't go as expected. What did you actually do?
options: Changed my approach on the spot
Asked for input and adjusted
Held my ground and it worked out
Let it go and focused on what I could do

text: Think of one moment today where things didn't go as expected. Looking back, was there a choice available to you — even a small one?
options: Yes — I see it now, even if I didn't then
Maybe — it's hard to tell
Not really — the situation was out of my hands
I don't think it would have mattered
answer=Changed my approach on the spot|Asked for input and adjusted|Held my ground and it worked out|Let it go and focused on what I could do:A1_R_INT
answer=Yes — I see it now, even if I didn't then|Maybe — it's hard to tell:A1_R_INT_SOFT
answer=Not really — the situation was out of my hands|I don't think it would have mattered:A1_R_EXT

text:
You stayed in the driver's seat today — even when the road changed.

That's not luck. Noticing your own agency, and using it, is a practice. You did it today.

Even if it wasn't clear in the moment — you can see now that a choice existed.

That gap between 'it happened to me' and 'I responded' — noticing it is where agency begins.

Hard days pull focus outward — to what others did, what circumstances did, what didn't come through.

Fair enough. But somewhere in today, even quietly — you made a call. Let that sit for a moment.
Now let's shift from how you handled today — to what you brought to it.
Think about your interactions today. Which of these feels most true?
options: I was mostly giving — energy, help, attention
I was mostly receiving — support, direction, resources
It was balanced — give and take
I was transacting — doing my part, no more
answer=I was mostly giving — energy, help, attention|It was balanced — give and take:A2_Q_CONTRIB
answer=I was mostly receiving — support, direction, resources|I was transacting — doing my part, no more:A2_Q_ENTITLE

text: Can you think of something you did today that wasn't strictly required of you?
options: Yes — I helped someone with something outside my scope
Yes — I flagged a problem I didn't have to flag
Sort of — I stayed longer or went deeper than expected
Not really — I focused on my own list

text: At any point today, did you feel like your effort wasn't being seen or rewarded fairly?
options: Yes — I felt underrecognized for what I put in
Yes — others got credit that I deserved
A little, but I let it go
No — I wasn't focused on that today
answer=Yes — I helped someone with something outside my scope|Yes — I flagged a problem I didn't have to flag|Sort of — I stayed longer or went deeper than expected:A2_Q_MOTIVE
answer=Not really — I focused on my own list:A2_Q_OPPORTUNITY
answer=Yes — I felt underrecognized for what I put in|Yes — others got credit that I deserved:A2_R_ENTITLE
answer=A little, but I let it go|No — I wasn't focused on that today:A2_Q_MOTIVE

text: When you gave that effort — what was underneath it?
options: I genuinely wanted to help
It felt like the right thing to do
I hoped it would be noticed
Habit — I just do these things

text: Looking back at today — was there a moment where someone could have used a hand, and you didn't step in?
options: Yes — I saw it but was too busy
Yes — I noticed but wasn't sure if it was my place
Maybe — I'm not certain
No — I don't think so

text:
Giving without keeping score is rarer than it sounds.

What you brought today — the help, the care, the extra effort — that's the kind of thing that doesn't show up in any metric. But it shapes everything.

Wanting recognition isn't weakness — it's human.

But here's the question worth sitting with: when you focus on what you deserve, does it make you give more — or less? That shift, from receiving to giving, is where energy comes from.

Not every opportunity to give will be taken. That's fine.

Just noticing the ones you passed — without guilt — is the beginning of a different kind of day tomorrow.

You've looked at how you responded today, and what you gave.

Now — let's zoom out. Who else was in today?
When you think about today's biggest challenge — whose experience comes to mind first?
options: Mine — it was mine to carry
My team's — we were all in it
A specific colleague who had it harder
The people we're ultimately here to serve
answer=Mine — it was mine to carry:A3_Q_SELF
answer=My team's — we were all in it|A specific colleague who had it harder:A3_Q_OTHER
answer=The people we're ultimately here to serve:A3_Q_WIDE

text: That makes sense — it was your day, your challenge. Did anyone else's experience cross your mind at any point?
options: Yes — I thought about how it affected someone on my team
Briefly — but I was too absorbed in my own situation
Not really — I was heads down
I'm not sure

text: You thought about "{A3_OPEN.answer}". Did that awareness change how you acted?
options: Yes — I checked in or offered help
Yes — I adjusted my own approach because of it
I noticed, but didn't act on it
Not consciously — it just shifted my mood

text: Thinking about the end user or the larger purpose — did that shift something for you today?
options: Yes — it reminded me why the work matters
Yes — it made a frustrating task feel meaningful
I thought about it but it felt abstract
Not today — I was too close to the day-to-day
answer=Yes — I thought about how it affected someone on my team:A3_R_EXPAND
answer=Briefly — but I was too absorbed in my own situation|Not really — I was heads down|I'm not sure:A3_R_SELF
answer=Yes — I checked in or offered help|Yes — I adjusted my own approach because of it:A3_R_OTHER
answer=I noticed, but didn't act on it|Not consciously — it just shifted my mood:A3_R_EXPAND
answer=Yes — it reminded me why the work matters|Yes — it made a frustrating task feel meaningful:A3_R_TRANSCEND
answer=I thought about it but it felt abstract|Not today — I was too close to the day-to-day:A3_R_EXPAND

text:
Being absorbed in your own experience isn't selfishness — sometimes the problem demands all of you.

But here's something worth noticing: the days that feel most draining are often the most self-contained ones. Meaning tends to live at the edge where your world meets someone else's.

You caught a glimpse of someone else's day inside your own.

That small act of looking outward — even briefly — is what keeps work from becoming a closed loop. You didn't just survive your day. You were present in someone else's.

You saw someone else clearly today — and that changed what you did.

That's perspective-taking in action. Not as a concept, but as a behaviour. The team feels it, even when no one says anything.

You touched the largest frame today — the person at the end of all this work.

Maslow called this self-transcendence: the shift from 'what do I need?' to 'what does the world need from me?' It's not abstract. Today, you felt it.

options: I want to carry more agency tomorrow
I want to give more than I take
I want to widen my circle of awareness
I'll hold what today showed me

text:
Good. That's enough for tonight.

Rest well. Tomorrow is another chance.
